name: 2023-07-basin
note: Basin (Beanstalk Wells AMM + MultiFlow pump oracle). Representative High/Medium set grounded to the clone's source lines.

known_findings.summary: storeUint128's odd-reserve branch re-reads the target slot's high 128 bits (shr(128, sload(...))) and re-packs them; with certain reserve counts/layouts this clobbers or mis-preserves an adjacent packed reserve, corrupting stored well reserves (low-level packing logic, no modeled detector).

The MultiFlow geometric-mean pump derives reserves consumers use as an oracle; a single large swap right before reading (and the first/seeded update) can move the reported reserves, so an integrating protocol reading the pump can be price-manipulated.

update() drives the EMA/cumulative reserves off block.timestamp deltas cast to uint40; the weighting depends on the exact timing of updates, so a party controlling when update() is called can bias the time-weighted reserves.

calcReserve recovers a reserve via an integer square root; the rounding direction of the sqrt/division can round in favor of the swapper rather than the pool, leaking small amounts from LPs over many swaps (precision/rounding-direction).

The pump's first update (_init) seeds lastReserves from whatever the well reserves are at that block; an attacker can set the initial cumulative/last reserves to a skewed value, biasing early TWA reads (protocol-specific initialization accounting).
